results.title: Artificial Intelligence | Product Hunt
results.content:
Image 114: Rankfender Rankfender

AI apps are no longer apps. They are attachments to surfaces you already use.
Last week, six AI products launched on Product Hunt that share one move. None of them ask users to open a new app. They embed into surfaces people already touch.

Hardware: Dune Keypad (46 upvotes) sits next to your keyboard with Claude integration. Video calls: Mina Meeting Assistant (47 upvotes). Text threads: folk (51 upvotes). Chat windows: Databox MCP (39 upvotes) plugs business data into Claude via Model Context Protocol. Mac autocomplete: Typeahead (22 upvotes). results.title: Beautiful.ai: Create beautiful presentations 10x faster | Product Hunt
results.content:
3.0

Based on 9 reviews

Review Beautiful.ai?

Beautiful.ai receives mixed reviews from users. Some praise its simplicity and time-saving features, highlighting its ease of use and ability to streamline presentation creation. However, many users express dissatisfaction, citing issues with clunky functionality, poor customer service, and billing problems. Several users report difficulties with cancellations and unexpected charges, leading to frustration. While some find it a helpful PowerPoint alternative, others feel it underdelivers and lacks value. Overall, Beautiful.ai's reception is polarized, with significant concerns about its service and reliability.

I had the same experience as others here. The tool is very clunky and I immediately decided it provides no additional value to me than PowerPoint. I could not find the cancellation option. They charged my credit card and refused to refund even though I contacted them minutes after they automatically charged.

results.title: The best AI agents in 2026 | Product Hunt
results.content:
Sources:Image 103: Tony CantafioreviewImage 104: Craig HarmanreviewImage 105: Jason Lairdreview

Recent launches

Recent AI agent launches skew toward practical workflow automation, with products spanning personal context capture, design execution, and governed enterprise operations. AirJelly focuses on private, on-device memory and task extraction across apps, while Genspark Design and Adapt emphasize turning prompts into usable assets, code, analytics, and cross-tool actions with stronger sharing, control, and deployment features."
